expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns elsewhere in this repository instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: The response references snapshot feature-dev plus live feature-dev, create-plan, create-spec, and create-tasks patterns.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.

expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: The artifact includes implementation slices, file-by-file map, and validation plan in a template-like structure.

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: final_response.md includes an Artifact Status section and exact artifact path.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: The response discusses strictness trade-offs and enforcement scope across tracks.

notes: Whether strict enforcement should apply to Light track remains unresolved.

expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns elsewhere in this repository instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: The response references feature-dev, handoff template, create-plan, create-tasks, and code-architect patterns in this repo.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.
The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: The artifact includes sections for Implementation Slices, File-by-File Implementation Map, and Validation Plan, along with summary, goals, findings, assumptions, and kickoff steps.

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: final_response.md has an Artifact Status section with the exact absolute path to the created handoff plan.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: The response includes policy trade-offs such as enforcement overhead versus reliability and questions about standard-track strictness.

notes: Whether Standard track should enforce artifact creation even when implementation completes in same turn remains open.
This benchmark includes only 2 runs total (1 old_skill and 1 with_skill for eval_id 2), so variance and stability cannot be assessed.
All 5 expectations pass in both configurations, so current assertions still do not differentiate quality between old_skill and with_skill on this eval.
with_skill uses fewer tokens than old_skill in this sample: 2636 vs 4520 (1884 fewer, about 41.7% lower).
with_skill also uses fewer tool calls than old_skill: 14 vs 18 (4 fewer, about 22.2% lower).
time_seconds is 0.0 in both runs, so this iteration provides no usable latency signal.
Both runs report 0 errors, indicating equivalent reliability for this single sampled eval.
